perturbation.original_text: WHEREAS , SUPPLIER is interested in receivi ng certain Confidential Information (as defined herein) from GKT in order to enable SUPPLIER to evaluate the possibility of acting as GK's supplier for one or more parts or work packages for the GK’s application (the “Purpose”).
perturbation.changed_text: WHEREAS , VENDOR is interested in receivi ng certain Confidential Information (as defined herein) from GKT in order to enable VENDOR to evaluate the possibility of acting as GK's supplier for one or more parts or work packages for the GK’s application (the “Purpose”).
perturbation.explanation: The term 'SUPPLIER' is changed to 'VENDOR' in the 'WHEREAS' clause. This introduces a contradiction because throughout the rest of the agreement, the term 'SUPPLIER' is consistently used. This raises the question of whether 'VENDOR' and 'SUPPLIER' refer to the same entity, potentially invalidating the agreement or causing confusion regarding obligations. In the clause: “SUPPLIER agrees to hold all Confidential Information in strict confidence and will not disclose or use the Confidential Information for its benefit or the benefit of any other company or entity anywhere in the world or any other purpose other than for the Purpose set forth in the recitals herein.” It is unclear to whom it is referring.
perturbation.contradicted_text: SUPPLIER agrees to hold all Confidential Information in strict confidence and will not disclose or use the Confidential Information for its benefit or the benefit of any other company or entity anywhere in the world or any other purpose other than for the Purpose set forth in the recitals herein.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'SUPPLIER' to 'VENDOR' in the 'WHEREAS' clause creates a contradiction with the contradicted text, which uses 'SUPPLIER'. The explanation clearly states that this change introduces ambiguity regarding which entity the clause 'SUPPLIER agrees to hold all Confidential Information' refers to, potentially invalidating the agreement or causing confusion. This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' where the contract uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: Confidential Information may only be used by SUPPLIER in connection with the pursuance of the Purpose set forth in the recitals herein.
perturbation.changed_text: Confidential Information may only be used by the Subcontractor in connection with the pursuance of the Purpose set forth in the recitals herein.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'SUPPLIER' to 'Subcontractor' introduces ambiguity because the term 'Subcontractor' is not defined anywhere else in the agreement. This alteration introduces confusion because it isn't clear if the term is related to 'SUPPLIER' or another third-party, making it unclear to whom the obligations and restrictions around Confidential Information apply. In the clause: 'SUPPLIER shall maintain all Confidential Information received from GK in the same manner that the SUPPLIER maintains its own Confidential Information, provided that the standard of care required shall be at least a reasonable standard' it is unclear to whom it is referring.
perturbation.contradicted_text: SUPPLIER shall maintain all Confidential Information received from GK in the same manner that the SUPPLIER maintains its own Confidential Information, provided that the standard of care required shall be at least a reasonable standard. Information disclosed in other that written form shall be considered Confidential Information only to the extent GK summarizes the same in a written form that is transmitted to SUPPLIER within thirty (30) calendar days of the non-written disclosures.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'SUPPLIER' to 'Subcontractor' introduces a contradiction because 'Subcontractor' is undefined, creating ambiguity. The Contradicted Text specifies 'SUPPLIER' as the entity responsible for maintaining Confidential Information. The changed text introduces uncertainty as to whether the Subcontractor has any obligations or restrictions regarding the Confidential Information in the contradicted text, thus breaking compatibility and creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: SUPPLIER shall maintain all Confidential Information received from GK in the same manner that the SUPPLIER maintains its own Confidential Information, provided that the standard of care required shall be at least a reasonable standard.
perturbation.changed_text: SUPPLIER shall maintain all Confidential Information received from GK in the same manner that the PROVIDER maintains its own Confidential Information, provided that the standard of care required shall be at least a reasonable standard.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'SUPPLIER' has been changed to 'PROVIDER' within Section 4. 'PROVIDER' is not defined elsewhere. This creates confusion regarding the standard of care expected. It's unclear whose internal practices are relevant, creating an in-text contradiction in the same location by reffering to 'SUPPLIER' in another context. Is it the standard of the supplier named as SUPPLIER, or the standard of care of an undefined 'PROVIDER'?
perturbation.contradicted_text: SUPPLIER shall maintain all Confidential Information received from GK in the same manner that the PROVIDER maintains its own Confidential Information, provided that the standard of care required shall be at least a reasonable standard. Information disclosed in other that written form shall be considered Confidential Information only to the extent GK summarizes the same in a written form that is transmitted to SUPPLIER within thirty (30) calendar days of the non-written disclosures.
perturbation.justification: The change replaces 'SUPPLIER' with 'PROVIDER', which is not defined. This creates ambiguity. The Contradicted Text references both SUPPLIER and PROVIDER, which means there's a direct contradiction since the Changed Text introduces a term not previously defined, and the Contradicted Text uses both, making it unclear whose obligations are being referenced.

perturbation.original_text: SUPPLIER agrees to hold all Confidential Information in strict confidence and will not disclose or use the Confidential Information for its benefit or the benefit of any other company or entity anywhere in the world or any other purpose other than for the Purpose set forth in the recitals herein.
perturbation.changed_text: SUPPLIER agrees to treat all Confidential Data with reasonable care and will not share or utilize the Confidential Information for any reason other than as stated in the recitals.
perturbation.explanation: The phrase 'hold all Confidential Information in strict confidence' is changed to 'treat all Confidential Data with reasonable care.' Additionally, 'disclose or use the Confidential Information for its benefit or the benefit of any other company or entity anywhere in the world or any other purpose' has been changed to 'share or utilize the Confidential Information for any reason.' The term 'Data' is not defined and the text, in another location uses the word 'Information'. This creates a contradiction because 'reasonable care' is less stringent than 'strict confidence', and 'share or utilize' is less comprehensive than the original phrase. As for example, clause 6 says: All Confidential Information is and shall remain the property of GK.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All Confidential Information is and shall remain the property of GK. At any time that GKT may request, SUPPLIER shall return the Confidential Information to GK and shall certify in writing that all copies thereof in its possession have been destroyed.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'hold all Confidential Information in strict confidence' to 'treat all Confidential Data with reasonable care' introduces a contradiction. 'Reasonable care' is a lower standard than 'strict confidence', and the term 'Data' is not defined while other clauses reference 'Information', which directly conflicts with the contradicted text stating 'All Confidential Information is and shall remain the property of GK'. This creates ambiguity and makes enforcement of the original confidentiality terms unclear, as the SUPPLIER's obligation is now less stringent and refers to a potentially different scope of information.

perturbation.original_text: Any disclosure of Confidential Inform ation shall be limited SUPPLIER’s employees and who have a need to use or study such Confidential Information for the Purpose.
perturbation.changed_text: Any disclosure of Protected Information shall be limited SUPPLIER’s personnel and who have a need to use or study such Protected Information for the Purpose.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Confidential Information' is replaced with 'Protected Information', while the term 'employees' is replaced with 'personnel.' These changes introduce uncertainty because 'Protected Information' and 'personnel' are not defined. This creates confusion whether these refer to the same scope of information and individuals as the original terms or if it is something else. Also, at the beginning of the text, there is a clause saying: “Confidential Information” means all trade secrets and information disclosed by GK to SUPPLIER, including, but not limited to, type design data, drawings, photographs, specifications, models, prototypes, designs, materials, construction or assembly, computer hardware and softwa re (whether in machine-re adable or human-readable form), technical, commercial and operational information concerning products, information concerning manufacturing methods and techniques, quality control and test methods, marketing data including target customers, customer lists and market plans, cost and pricing data and product applications.
perturbation.contradicted_text: “Confidential Information” means all trade secrets and information disclosed by GK to SUPPLIER, including, but not limited to, type design data, drawings, photographs, specifications, models, prototypes, designs, materials, construction or assembly, computer hardware and softwa re (whether in machine-re adable or human-readable form), technical, commercial and operational information concerning products, information concerning manufacturing methods and techniques, quality control and test methods, marketing data including target customers, customer lists and market plans, cost and pricing data and product applications.
perturbation.justification: The original text uses the clearly defined term "Confidential Information." The changed text replaces this with "Protected Information," which is undefined. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text' as it introduces a different term which is not defined in the section, whereas the 'Confidential Information' is clearly defined. Therefore, it creates a strong in-text contradiction as per the Misaligned Terminology definition. This creates uncertainty and potential legal issues because the scope of what is being protected has been changed.

perturbation.original_text: All Confidential Information is and shall remain the property of GK. At any time that GKT may request, SUPPLIER shall return the Confidential Information to GK and shall certify in writing that all copies thereof in its possession have been destroyed.
perturbation.changed_text: All sensitive data is and shall remain the property of GK. At any time that GKT may request, SUPPLIER shall return the Confidential Information to GK and shall certify in writing that all copies thereof in its possession have been destroyed.
perturbation.explanation: The phrase 'Confidential Information' is replaced with 'sensitive data',. This change introduces uncertainty because 'sensitive data' is not defined. This creates confusion whether these refer to the same scope of information, while the term 'Confidential Information' is, defined earlier in section 1. This could affect the enforcement of the returning obligations if it is not clear what is to be returned to GK. As for example, clause 1 says: “Confidential Information” means all trade secrets and information disclosed by GK to SUPPLIER, including, but not limited to, type design data, drawings, photographs, specifications, models, prototypes, designs, materials, construction or assembly, computer hardware and softwa re (whether in machine-re adable or human-readable form), technical, commercial and operational information concerning products, information concerning manufacturing methods and techniques, quality control and test methods, marketing data including target customers, customer lists and market plans, cost and pricing data and product applications.
perturbation.contradicted_text: “Confidential Information” means all trade secrets and information disclosed by GK to SUPPLIER, including, but not limited to, type design data, drawings, photographs, specifications, models, prototypes, designs, materials, construction or assembly, computer hardware and softwa re (whether in machine-re adable or human-readable form), technical, commercial and operational information concerning products, information concerning manufacturing methods and techniques, quality control and test methods, marketing data including target customers, customer lists and market plans, cost and pricing data and product applications.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'sensitive data'. The term 'Confidential Information' is defined elsewhere in the contract, while 'sensitive data' is not. This creates ambiguity as to whether the same scope of information is meant in the returning obligations, directly contradicting the definition provided in the 'Contradicted Text' section. The supplier would not know whether to use the definition provided or use a common sense interpretation of 'sensitive data' instead.
